description:
You are a QA engineer who has just collected multiple execution logs from an automated regression run.  
All raw logs have already been copied into

/home/user/test_results/raw_logs

where every file has a “.log” extension.  
Each log-line that describes an executed test case follows this exact, single-line pattern:

[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] TESTCASE: <test-case-name> RESULT: <PASS | FAIL> TIME: <floating-point-seconds>

Goal  
1. Create the directory /home/user/test_results/summary (if it does not yet exist).  
2. Use **awk** and **sed** (no other scripting language) to build a consolidated, human-readable summary file that must be saved exactly as

/home/user/test_results/summary/qa_summary_20240101.txt

3. Extract every line that contains the word “ERROR” (case-sensitive) from all raw log files, remove duplicates while preserving original order, and store the result in

/home/user/test_results/summary/error_extract.log



==========  SUMMARY FILE SPECIFICATION (qa_summary_20240101.txt)  ==========
The automated checker will verify the summary file line-by-line, so follow the
format below exactly—spacing, capitalisation and blank lines included.

Line 1:  QA TEST SUMMARY  
Line 2:  (must be completely blank)  

Line 3:  TESTCASE | RESULT | TIME(sec)  

Lines 4 … N: one line per unique test case, alphabetically sorted
           by the literal test-case-name (a-z).  Every such line must be in
           the form

           <test-case-name> | <PASS | FAIL> | <time-value-as-appeared>

           • Use the **latest** occurrence of the test case across *all*
             logs when multiple runs exist.  
           • Preserve the floating-point TIME exactly as it appears
             in that latest occurrence.  

After the last test-case line, insert one blank line, then append:

TOTAL_PASS: <integer-count>  
TOTAL_FAIL: <integer-count>  
OVERALL STATUS: <PASS | FAIL>  

If TOTAL_FAIL is zero, “OVERALL STATUS” must be PASS; otherwise FAIL.  

Finally, add one more line:

GENERATED_AT: <ISO-8601-UTC-timestamp>

Use `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` (or equivalent) so that the timestamp is in
UTC and matches the exact format 2024-01-01T13:37:42Z.

No other content, headers, footers or trailing whitespace is allowed.



==========  ERROR LOG SPECIFICATION (error_extract.log)  ==========
• Each line must be copied verbatim from the original raw logs.  
• Keep the chronological order in which the lines first appear
  when log files are scanned in lexical filename order (e.g. run1.log,
  run2.log, …).  
• Remove duplicates: a line that is byte-for-byte identical to an
  earlier one must not appear again.  
• End the file with a single trailing newline and no extra blank lines.



==========  REQUIREMENTS & HINTS  ==========
• Only **awk** and **sed** are allowed for text extraction, cleaning and
  transforming.  You may combine them with standard utilities such as
  `sort`, `uniq`, `mkdir`, `cat`, `grep`, etc.  
• Do not edit the raw log files themselves.  
• Make sure permissions allow the current unprivileged user to create and
  write the summary directory and files.  
• Always overwrite qa_summary_20240101.txt and error_extract.log if they
  already exist, so the task is idempotent.  
• The automated tester will:
  1. Verify that the summary and error files exist at the exact paths.
  2. Check that the summary file strictly follows the format above,
     that every unique test case appearing in the logs is present,
     that only the latest occurrence was considered, that the list is
     alphabetical, and that PASS/FAIL counts and overall status are correct.
  3. Confirm that error_extract.log contains every and only the expected
     ERROR lines, in the correct order, without duplicates.

You may display intermediate console output while working, but the two files
described above are the **only** artefacts that will be examined for grading.
truth:
Initial fixture (pre-existing on disk before the agent starts):

Directory tree:
/home/user/test_results/raw_logs
├── run1.log
├── run2.log
└── run3.log

run1.log
[2024-01-01 10:00:00] INFO Starting test suite
[2024-01-01 10:00:01] TESTCASE: login_functionality RESULT: PASS TIME: 1.2
[2024-01-01 10:00:02] TESTCASE: payment_gateway RESULT: FAIL TIME: 3.8
[2024-01-01 10:00:03] INFO Suite finished

run2.log
[2024-01-01 11:00:00] INFO Starting test suite
[2024-01-01 11:00:01] TESTCASE: search_functionality RESULT: PASS TIME: 0.9
[2024-01-01 11:00:02] TESTCASE: checkout_process RESULT: PASS TIME: 2.5
[2024-01-01 11:00:03] ERROR Unexpected timeout in payment_gateway
[2024-01-01 11:00:04] INFO Suite finished

run3.log
[2024-01-01 12:00:00] INFO Starting test suite
[2024-01-01 12:00:01] TESTCASE: user_profile RESULT: PASS TIME: 1.7
[2024-01-01 12:00:02] TESTCASE: password_reset RESULT: PASS TIME: 2.1
[2024-01-01 12:00:03] INFO Suite finished


Expected content of /home/user/test_results/summary/qa_summary_20240101.txt (except for the GENERATED_AT timestamp which is allowed to vary, but must match the regex
^GENERATED_AT: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$):

QA TEST SUMMARY

TESTCASE | RESULT | TIME(sec)
checkout_process | PASS | 2.5
login_functionality | PASS | 1.2
password_reset | PASS | 2.1
payment_gateway | FAIL | 3.8
search_functionality | PASS | 0.9
user_profile | PASS | 1.7

TOTAL_PASS: 5
TOTAL_FAIL: 1
OVERALL STATUS: FAIL
GENERATED_AT: <timestamp matches regex above>

Expected content of /home/user/test_results/summary/error_extract.log:

[2024-01-01 11:00:03] ERROR Unexpected timeout in payment_gateway

(terminated by a single newline)

All tests will be performed with these exact source logs, so any hard-coded
assumptions on filenames and contents that match the above will pass.
